message:
fix(packs/containers): close safe-pattern bypass on ps/logs/build in container name

docker/podman safe patterns matched `\bps\b`, `\blogs\b`, `\bbuild\b`,
etc. on both sides of the keyword — so when a container name contained
one of those subcommand words as a substring, the safe pattern would
short-circuit and bypass the destructive rule:

  docker rm -f ps-container     -> matched `docker-ps` safe (via ps in
                                    ps-container), skipped rm-force
  docker rmi -f build-server    -> matched `docker-build` safe
  docker volume rm logs-archive -> matched `docker-logs` safe

Changed the trailing `\b` to `(?=\s|$)` on every single-token safe
subcommand across docker and podman packs. Real invocations (`docker ps`,
`docker logs mycontainer`, `docker build -t app .`) still short-circuit.

Regression test added for all three shapes plus the positive cases.
